#[derive(Debug, sqlx::FromRow)]
pub struct GetUserByIdRow {
    pub id: i32,
    pub name: String,
    pub email: Option<String>,
    pub status: UserStatus,
    pub created_at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
}

pub async fn get_user_by_id(
    pool: &sqlx::PgPool,
    id: i32,
) -> Result<GetUserByIdRow, sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query_as!(
        GetUserByIdRow,
        "SELECT id, name, email, status AS \"status: UserStatus\", \
         created_at FROM users WHERE id = $1",
        id
    )
    .fetch_one(pool)
    .await
}

#[derive(Debug, sqlx::FromRow)]
pub struct ListActiveUsersRow {
    pub id: i32,
    pub name: String,
    pub email: Option<String>,
}

pub async fn list_active_users(
    pool: &sqlx::PgPool,
    status: &UserStatus,
) -> Result<Vec<ListActiveUsersRow>, sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query_as!(
        ListActiveUsersRow,
        "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE status = $1",
        status as &UserStatus
    )
    .fetch_all(pool)
    .await
}

pub async fn update_user_email(
    pool: &sqlx::PgPool,
    email: &str,
    id: i32,
) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    sqlx::query!(
        "UPDATE users SET email = $1 WHERE id = $2",
        email, id
    )
    .execute(pool)
    .await?;
    Ok(())
}
